hypothesis: In MADE experiments on held-out ternary chemical systems with a 20-query budget, an agent that uses an LLM-guided generative proposer, an ensemble-MLIP to estimate epistemic uncertainty, and a two-stage multi-fidelity evaluation pipeline (MLIP screening followed by selective DFT on high-uncertainty/high-promise candidates) will discover at least twice as many novel thermodynamically stable compounds (per convex-hull criterion) as a baseline MatterGen+diversity acquisition agent using MLIP-only oracles.
